what: Low, medium and high cloud description
title: Code Tables
code: # From Code Table 0513 low_cloud_codes <- c(   "0" = "No CL clouds",   "1" = "Cumulus humilis or cumulus fractus other than of bad weather,* or both",   "2" = "Cumulus mediocris or congestus, with or without cumulus of species fractus or humilis or stratocumulus, all having their bases at the same level",   "3" = "Cumulonimbus calvus, with or without cumulus, stratocumulus or Stratus",   "4" = "Stratocumulus cumulogenitus",   "5" = "Stratocumulus other than stratocumulus cumulogenitus",   "6" = "Stratus nebulosus or Stratus fractus other than of bad weather,* or both",   "7" = "Stratus fractus or cumulus fractus of bad weather,* or both (pannus), usually below altostratus or nimbostratus",   "8" = "Cumulus and stratocumulus other than stratocumulus cumulogenitus, with bases at different levels",   "9" = "Cumulonimbus capillatus (often with an anvil), with or without cumulonimbus calvus, cumulus, stratocumulus, Stratus or pannus",   "/" = "CL clouds invisible owing to darkness, fog, blowing dust or sand, or other similar phenomena" )  # From Code Table 0515 medium_cloud_codes <- c(   "0" = "No CM clouds",   "1" = "Altostratus translucidus",   "2" = "Altostratus opacus or nimbostratus",   "3" = "Altocumulus translucidus at a single level",   "4" = "Patches (often lenticular) of altocumulus translucidus, continually changing and occurring at one or more levels",   "5" = "Altocumulus translucidus in bands, or one or more layers of altocumulus translucidus or opacus, progressively invading the sky; these altocumulus clouds generally thicken as a whole",   "6" = "Altocumulus cumulogenitus (or cumulonimbogenitus)",   "7" = "Altocumulus translucidus or opacus in two or more layers, or altocumulus opacus in a single layer, not progressively invading the sky, or altocumulus with altostratus or nimbostratus",   "8" = "Altocumulus castellanus or floccus",   "9" = "Altocumulus of a chaotic sky, generally at several levels",   "/" = "CM clouds invisible owing to darkness, fog, blowing dust or sand, or other similar phenomena, or because of continuous layer of lower clouds" )  # From Code Table 0509 high_cloud_codes <- c(   "0" = "No CH clouds",   "1" = "Cirrus fibratus, sometimes uncinus, not progressively invading the sky",   "2" = "Cirrus spissatus, in patches or entangled sheaves, which usually do not increase and sometimes seem to be the remains of the upper part of a cumulonimbus; or cirrus castellanus or floccus",   "3" = "Cirrus spissatus cumulonimbogenitus",   "4" = "Cirrus uncinus or fibratus, or both, progressively invading the sky; they generally thicken as a whole",   "5" = "Cirrus (often in bands) and cirrostratus, or cirrostratus alone, progressively invading the sky; they generally thicken as a whole, but the continuous veil does not reach 45 degrees above the horizon",   "6" = "Cirrus (often in bands) and cirrostratus, or cirrostratus alone, progressively invading the sky; they generally thicken as a whole; the continuous veil extends more than 45 degrees above the horizon, without the sky being totally covered",   "7" = "Cirrostratus covering the whole sky",   "8" = "Cirrostratus not progressively invading the sky and not entirely covering it",   "9" = "Cirrocumulus alone, or cirrocumulus predominant among the CH clouds",   "/" = "CH clouds invisible owing to darkness, fog, blowing dust or sand, or other similar phenomena, or because of a continuous layer of lower clouds" )

title: Extracted data reference
text: support sections 222 y 444. lead wrong result function crack filtered . Section 555 (reserved national distribution) quietly removed present, content varies country. Group 54 section 3 (temperature change), well groups 9 section 1 3, ignored won’t affect results.

title: Introducing synopR
text: show_synop_data() package’s core function. requires character vector data frame column element SYNOP string. ’s vectorized, large vectors can processed seconds. , first , SYNOP messages checked check_synop(). function make sure every message starts “AAXX” ends “=”, contain invalid characters (valid characters removing “AAXX” “=” digits 0-9, ‘/’ ‘NIL’), verifies groups consist 5 digits (except section identifiers ‘333’ ‘555’). return data frame two columns: boolean column indicating validity (can used filter ), second one pointing possible errors. columns associated information present SYNOP messages removed default. reason don’t want , set remove_empty_cols = FALSE. optional wmo_identifier argument allows automatic filtering case data contains messages different stations. working thousands SYNOP strings multiple stations, built-filtering becomes extremely convenient. complete detailed table meaning details columns returned show_synop_data() available vignette “Extracted data reference”.
code: library(synopR)  # Notice that the second SYNOP will be removed because of the incomplete group '8127' data_input_vector <- c("AAXX 04003 87736 32965 00000 10204 20106 39982 40074 5//// 333 10266 20158 =",                        "AAXX 03183 87736 32965 12708 10254 20052 30005 40098 5//// 80005 333 56000 8127 =",                        "AAXX 03183 87736 32965 12708 10254 20052 30005 40098 5//// 80005 333 56000 81270 =")  checked <- check_synop(data_input_vector) my_data <- show_synop_data(data_input_vector[checked$is_valid == TRUE])  knitr::kable(t(my_data)) library(synopR) # Messages from 87736 and 87016 mixed_synop <- c("AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818=",                  "AAXX 04033 87016 41460 83208 10200 20194 39712 40114 50003 70292 888// 333 56699 82810 88615="                  )  colorado_data <- show_synop_data(mixed_synop, wmo_identifier = '87736', remove_empty_cols = TRUE) #> Warning in show_synop_data(mixed_synop, wmo_identifier = "87736", #> remove_empty_cols = TRUE): 1 message(s) do not contain the identifier '87736' #> and will be discarded. knitr::kable(t(colorado_data))

title: Introducing synopR
text: Ogimet known respectable source SYNOP messages. download_from_ogimet() can used download SYNOP messages webpage. need WMO identifier station interest. period interest can’t longer 370 days. aware result contain prefixes added Ogimet, information regarding WMO id date. However, issue, can employ parse_ogimet(). tool designed separate aggregates raw SYNOP message processing (show_synop_data() make use aggregates add columns ‘Year’ ‘Month’). , didn’t make use check_synop(). , must said data frame multiple columns —SYNOP column explicitly specified— accepted data frame direct output parse_ogimet(). steps (download, parse, check decode) included one single function, direct_download_from_ogimet(), return direct decoded result.
code: library(synopR)  # Suppose we have downloaded this data with: # download_from_ogimet("87736","2026-02-01","2026-02-01") data_input <- data.frame(synops = c("87736,2026,02,01,03,00,AAXX 01034 87736 NIL=",                                     "87736,2026,02,01,06,00,AAXX 01064 87736 NIL=",                                     "87736,2026,02,01,09,00,AAXX 01094 87736 NIL=",                                     "87736,2026,02,01,12,00,AAXX 01123 87736 12965 31808 10240 20210 39992 40082 5//// 60104 82075 333 10282 20216 56055 82360=",                                     "87736,2026,02,01,15,00,AAXX 01154 87736 NIL=",                                     "87736,2026,02,01,18,00,AAXX 01183 87736 12465 20000 10326 20215 39974 40064 5//// 60001 82100 333 56600 82818=",                                     "87736,2026,02,01,21,00,AAXX 01214 87736 NIL="))  # Note that `parse_ogimet(data_input)` is incorrect data_from_ogimet <- parse_ogimet(data_input$synops)   # 'Year' and 'Month' column are included! # 'NIL' messages are ignored parse_ogimet(data_input$synops) |> show_synop_data() |> t() |> knitr::kable() #> Warning in show_synop_data(parse_ogimet(data_input$synops)): 5 NIL messages #> detected and removed.

title: Fast Decoding of SYNOP (Surface Synoptic Observations) Meteorological Messages
text: goal synopR provide simple fast tool decoding FM 12 SYNOP (Report surface observation fixed land station) messages, following WMO standards (World Meteorological Organization (WMO). Manual Codes (WMO-. 306), Volume .1. Geneva, 2019.). focuses extracting data sections 0, 1 3. synopR dependency-free! R (>= 4.1.0) needed.

title: Fast Decoding of SYNOP (Surface Synoptic Observations) Meteorological Messages

title: Fast Decoding of SYNOP (Surface Synoptic Observations) Meteorological Messages
text: 50 meteorological parameters can obtained just seconds. detailed guide data extracted show_synop_data() available “Extracted_data_Reference” vignette. can check structural integrity SYNOP messages decoding: Download SYNOP messages Ogimet download_from_ogimet(), download, check decode direct_download_from_ogimet(). package includes parser specifically designed comma-separated format used Ogimet:

title: Fast Decoding of SYNOP (Surface Synoptic Observations) Meteorological Messages
text: show_synop_data(), core function, completely vectorized. means ’s super fast! 45k SYNOP messages decoded just 13 seconds.

title: Fast Decoding of SYNOP (Surface Synoptic Observations) Meteorological Messages
text: Sections: package support sections 222 (maritime data), 444 (data clouds base station level) 555 (data national use, quietly discarded). Time observation: Observations assumed occur time indicated Section 0 (Group 9 Section 1 currently ignored). Humidity: Group 2 (Section 1) contains dew point, relative humidity. Geopotential height: pressure levels 850, 700 500 hPa supported. Trace Precipitation: converted 0.01 (mm). Groups supported: Groups starting 54 9 Section 3 currently ignored.

title: Fast Decoding of SYNOP (Surface Synoptic Observations) Meteorological Messages
text: Feel free report issue may find: Github “NA introduced coercion” generally associated specific part SYNOP message incorrectly codified.

title: Direct download of meteorological data from Ogimet — direct_download_from_ogimet
text: requested period exceed 370 days. queries assume UTC time zone. returned data frame covers 00:00 UTC initial_date 23:00 UTC final_date, inclusive. many requests may trigger temporal blocks. station identifier starts 0 (zero), wmo_identifier must string (e.g., "06447").

title: Decode multiple SYNOP messages — show_synop_data
Decode multiple SYNOP messages — show_synop_data
text: data character vector, data frame column containing raw SYNOP strings, exact data frame returned parse_ogimet(). wmo_identifier 5-digit character string integer representing station WMO ID. NULL (default), messages decoded. remove_empty_cols Logical. columns containing NA values removed? Default TRUE.

title: Decode multiple SYNOP messages — show_synop_data
text: data frame row represents one observation time column decoded meteorological variable. wmo_id - WMO station identifier Year - (parse_ogimet()) Month - (parse_ogimet()) Day - informed Section 0 Hour - informed Section 0 Cloud_base_height - Lowest cloud base height, intervals Visibility - meters Total_cloud_cover - oktas, 9 means 'invisible' sky fog phenomenon Wind_direction - tens degree, 99 means 'variable wind direction' Wind_speed Wind_speed_unit - Either 'm/s' 'knots' Air_temperature - degrees Celsius Dew_point - degrees Celsius Relative_humidity - percentage Station_pressure - hPa MSLP_GH - Mean sea level pressure (hPa) geopotential height (gpm) Pressure_tendency - hPa Charac_pressure_tend - String, simplified decoding Precipitation_S1 - mm Precip_period_S1 - hours ('Precipitation_S1' fell last 'Precip_period_S1' hours) Present_weather - String, simplified decoding Past_weather1 - String, simplified decoding Past_weather2 - String, simplified decoding Cloud_amount_Nh - Cloud coverage low medium cloud, 'Total_cloud_cover' Low_clouds_CL - String, simplified decoding Medium_clouds_CM - String, simplified decoding High_clouds_CH - String, simplified decoding Max_temperature - degrees Celsius Min_temperature - degrees Celsius Ground_state - String, simplified decoding Ground_temperature - Integer, degrees Celsius Snow_ground_state - String, simplified decoding Snow_depth - cm Ev_Evt - Evaporation (ev) evapotranspiration (evt), mm Sunshine_daily - hours (generally previous civil day) Positive_Net_Rad_last_24h - J/cm^2 Negative_Net_Rad_last_24h - J/cm^2 Global_Solar_Rad_last_24h - J/cm^2 Diffused_Solar_Rad_last_24h - J/cm^2 Downward_LongWave_Rad_last_24h - J/cm^2 Upward_LongWave_Rad_last_24h - J/cm^2 ShortWave_Rad_last_24h - J/cm^2 Net_ShortWave_Rad_last_24h - J/cm^2 Direct_Solar_Rad_last_24h - J/cm^2 Sunshine_last_hour - hours Positive_Net_Rad_last_hour - kJ/m^2 Negative_Net_Rad_last_hour - kJ/m^2 Global_Solar_Rad_last_hour - kJ/m^2 Diffused_Solar_Rad_last_hour - kJ/m^2 Downward_LongWave_Rad_last_hour - kJ/m^2 Upward_LongWave_Rad_last_hour - kJ/m^2 ShortWave_Rad_last_hour - kJ/m^2 Net_ShortWave_Rad_last_hour - kJ/m^2 Direct_Solar_Rad_last_hour - kJ/m^2 Cloud_drift_direction - cardinal intercardinal directions "low - medium - high" clouds Cloud_elevation_direction - String indicating genera, direction elevation angle Pressure_change_last_24h - hPa Precipitation_S3 - mm Precip_period_S3 - hours ('Precipitation_S3' fell last 'Precip_period_S3' hours) Precipitation_last_24h - mm Cloud_layer_1 - String indicating cover, genera height Cloud_layer_2 - String indicating cover, genera height Cloud_layer_3 - String indicating cover, genera height Cloud_layer_4 - String indicating cover, genera height

title: Decode multiple SYNOP messages — show_synop_data
code: msg <- paste0("AAXX 01123 87736 32965 13205 10214 20143 ",               "30022 40113 5//// 80005 333 10236 20128 56000 81270=") synop_df <- data.frame(messages = msg) decoded_data <- show_synop_data(synop_df)

title: synopR 1.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-03 SynopR now dependency-free. R (>= 4.1.0) needed. show_synop_data() associated internal functions completely vectorized, meaning execution times dramatically reduced. SYNOP message now almost completely decoded show_synop_data(). groups 29UUU 9 section 1 groups 54 9 section 3 remain ignored. Argument “remove_empty_cols” default value show_synop_data() changed FALSE TRUE. New function download_from_ogimet() retrieve SYNOP messages Ogimet. New function direct_download_from_ogimet() download, check decode . New vignette named “Extracted data Reference”.

title: synopR 0.2.2
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-18 CRAN release argument “wmo_identifier” show_synop_data() now optional. Column “Sea_level_pressure” show_synop_data() changed “MSLP_GH” geopotential heights pressure levels 850, 700 500 hPa now supported Tests ~ 4000 SYNOP messages Argentina resulted improvement internal functions, now can better handle potential errors

title: synopR 0.2.0
text: Found SYNOP downloaded Ogimet end “==” instead “=”, now parse_ogimet() can fix . Also, show_synop_data() check_synop() now aware . Added new argument show_synop_data() named remove_empty_cols precisely removes empty columns. argument wmo_identifier show_synop_data() can either integer string. show_synop_data() generates two novel columns: ‘Wind_speed_unit’ ‘Relative_humidity’ (Magnus-Tetens Equation). Official WMO Tables conversion form vectors available. Fixed bug related internal function returning NULL string “=” character included input, instead removing . clear documentation.

title: synopR 0.1.0
text: Initial release.
